judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with comparable depth and evidence; Report 2 is slightly more thorough in Q1 (also calls out and validates the SMS claim, and flags the missing billing route) but the two are essentially equivalent in substance.

report_1.evidence: **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default."** — False. `clinic-portal/app/security/config.py` reads: `# Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic` / `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`. ... Must be removed or corrected. ... **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF."** — Not built. `clinic-portal/app/routers/visits.py` only implements `GET /visits` returning an empty list ... there is no export endpoint or PDF generation code anywhere in the repo. ... Must be removed.

report_2.evidence: **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default."** — False. `clinic-portal/app/security/config.py` reads: ... `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` ... This must be corrected or removed. **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF."** — Not implemented. `clinic-portal/app/routers/visits.py` has only a stub ... There is no export/PDF code anywhere in the app. This claim ... must be removed.

`before_scheduling.png` renders the text "SCHEDULING CALENDAR" and per `captures.json` was captured from route `/schedule`. `after_scheduling.png` renders the text "BILLING / INVOICES" and per `captures.json` was captured from route `/billing/invoices`. So the "after" side of the comparison is a capture of an unrelated billing/invoices page, not the redesigned scheduling calendar

report_1.evidence: `after_export.png` — referenced in the doc (`![after](img/after_export.png)`) but **does not exist** in `clinic-portal/docs/release/img/`. ... `unused_capture_07.png` — exists on disk (renders "PATIENT LIST") but is referenced neither in the markdown document nor in `captures.json`.

report_2.evidence: `after_export.png` — referenced in the doc (`![after](img/after_export.png)`) but **does not exist** in `docs/release/img/`. ... `unused_capture_07.png` — exists in `docs/release/img/` (renders "PATIENT LIST") but is **not referenced anywhere** in the doc and has no entry in `captures.json`.

report_1.evidence: **"Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** footer — this is an internal/process note ("draft," authoring-tool attribution) that has no place in a finished customer email. ... It should be stripped before send

report_2.evidence: **The trailing line "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** — this reads as an internal drafting note ... At minimum it should be removed before send
